title: Motor imagery + spatial attention dataset from Forenzo & He 2023
description: This dataset comprises EEG recordings from 25 healthy participants performing motor imagery and spatial attention tasks across five sessions. The study integrates simultaneous motor imagery (left and right hand) with overt spatial attention in an online brain-computer interface paradigm, featuring 64-channel recordings at 1000 Hz with cursor feedback. The dataset includes 1,875 trials collected during synchronized, laboratory-based BCI experiments designed to investigate the combined effects of motor imagery and spatial attention on EEG-based control. This is a derivative dataset based on the original study by Forenzo & He (2023).
methods_description: EEG data were acquired using a Neuroscan Quik-Cap 64-channel system with SynAmps 2/RT amplifier at 1000 Hz sampling rate. The electrode montage followed the standard 1005 placement with reference between Cz and CPz. Online filters included a 200 Hz lowpass filter and 60 Hz notch filter. Participants performed motor imagery tasks (left and right hand imagination) during continuous visual pursuit with cursor feedback in a synchronous, online BCI paradigm across 5 sessions with 3 runs per session.
